title: Pipe Insulation Materials & Specifications

mineral_wool.name: Mineral Wool (Rock Wool / Glass Wool)

glass_wool.name: Glass Wool
glass_wool.density: Lower density, lighter, good for general HVAC.

mineral_wool.temperature_range: -40°C to +300°C (rock wool up to 700°C for specialist grades)
applications: General thermal insulation to hot services

facing_types.plain: Unfaced — requires separate finishing (cladding, tape). Cheapest.
facing_types.foil_faced: Aluminium foil laminate — provides vapour resistance and clean finish. Standard for most M&E.
facing_types.canvas_faced: Woven glass tissue — for painting or where aesthetic finish needed.
facing_types.wire_netting: Galvanised wire netting over plain — external pipework, high-vibration areas.
facing_types.aluminium_cladding: Separate aluminium sheet cladding — external pipework, plant rooms, visible areas. Measured separately.

form.preformed_sections: Pre-formed half-round sections — snap around pipe. Standard for pipe insulation.
form.mat_roll: Flexible mat/roll — for wrapping around vessels, large-bore pipes, irregular shapes.
form.slab: Rigid slab — for flat surfaces, tank walls, equipment.

mineral_wool.fire_rating: Non-combustible (Euroclass A1 for unfaced rock wool). Excellent fire performance.
mineral_wool.cost_note: Most cost-effective insulation for hot services. Thicker sections needed compared to phenolic for same performance.

phenolic_foam.name: Phenolic Foam
applications: Chilled water pipework (with vapour barrier facing)

facing_types.foil_faced: Aluminium foil — standard. Provides vapour barrier and clean finish.
facing_types.tissue_faced: Glass tissue — for areas requiring painting.
facing_types.note: Foil facing provides adequate vapour resistance for most hot-service applications. For cold/chilled services, check if additional vapour sealing at joints is required.

phenolic_foam.form: Pre-formed half-round sections. Available in 1m lengths standard.
phenolic_foam.fire_rating: Euroclass B-s1,d0 (limited combustibility). Good fire performance but not A1.
phenolic_foam.advantage: THINNEST insulation for equivalent thermal performance. Ideal where space is limited (ceiling voids, risers, congested plant rooms).
phenolic_foam.cost_note: More expensive per metre than mineral wool. Justified by space saving and thinner profile. Approximately 1.5-2× mineral wool cost.

elastomeric_foam.name: Elastomeric Foam (Flexible Rubber)
elastomeric_foam.temperature_range: -50°C to +105°C (standard grades). Up to +150°C for high-temp grades.
applications: Domestic cold water (L8 compliance — prevent warming above 20°C)

vapour_barrier.description: Closed-cell structure provides INHERENT vapour barrier — no separate vapour barrier needed.
vapour_barrier.note: This is the KEY advantage for cold services — prevents condensation forming on cold pipe surface. Mineral wool does NOT have inherent vapour barrier.

facing_types.plain: Self-sealing — plain black rubber. Standard for concealed pipework.
facing_types.uv_resistant: UV-resistant outer skin — for exposed external applications.
facing_types.coloured: Coloured finish available (grey, white) — for exposed visible areas.

form.tube: Pre-slit tube sections — opens and wraps around pipe. Standard for pipe insulation.
form.sheet: Flat sheet — for wrapping valves, fittings, irregular shapes, equipment.
form.roll: Continuous roll — for large-bore pipes and vessels.

fire_rating.standard: Euroclass B-s3,d0 or C-s3,d0 depending on grade.
fire_rating.enhanced: Armaflex Ultima — Euroclass B-s1,d0. Enhanced fire grade for plenums and escape routes.
fire_rating.note: Standard elastomeric may not meet fire requirements in plenums, escape routes, or high-risk areas. Check spec for fire class requirement.

installation_notes: All joints MUST be glued with manufacturer adhesive — gaps allow condensation.
Do NOT compress elastomeric insulation against supports — reduces performance and creates cold bridges.
Vapour-sealed support inserts required at pipe clips/brackets on cold services.
Self-adhesive grades reduce installation time but are more expensive.
elastomeric_foam.cost_note: More expensive than mineral wool per metre. Essential for cold services due to integral vapour barrier. Approximately 2-3× mineral wool cost.

calcium_silicate.name: Calcium Silicate
calcium_silicate.form: Rigid pre-formed half-round sections and flat slabs. Heavy — requires robust support.
calcium_silicate.facing: Typically finished with aluminium cladding (measured separately) or canvas and paint.
calcium_silicate.cost_note: Significantly more expensive than mineral wool. Only used for high-temperature applications where mineral wool is insufficient.
calcium_silicate.flag_if: Calcium silicate specified — check if operating temperature actually requires it. Often over-specified where mineral wool would suffice.

pir.name: PIR (Polyisocyanurate)
pir.fire_rating: Euroclass B or C depending on grade and facing.
pir.note: Similar to phenolic in performance. Less common in UK M&E than phenolic (Kingspan Kooltherm dominates). Check spec for which is specified.
pir.cost_note: Similar cost to phenolic. Material choice often driven by spec preference.

thickness_tables.source: BS 5422 / Part L Building Regulations / TIMSA HVAC Guide
thickness_tables.rule: These are MINIMUM thicknesses for compliance. Specification may require MORE than minimum. Always check spec first — these tables are the FALLBACK when spec is silent.

lthw_heating.general_rule: For pipes >54mm: approximately pipe diameter ÷ 2 as minimum insulation thickness. Always verify against BS 5422 table.
lthw_heating.note: Phenolic foam thicknesses may be thinner than mineral wool for same performance due to lower thermal conductivity. Check manufacturer data.

dhw_hot_water.system: DHWS (Domestic Hot Water Supply, 60°C storage, 55°C distribution)
dhw_hot_water.note: DHW insulation must maintain distribution temperature above 50°C at outlets (L8 ACOP). Dead legs must be minimised. Secondary return must be insulated.

cws_cold_water.system: CWS (Cold Water Supply — prevent warming above 20°C per L8)
cws_cold_water.material_default: Elastomeric foam (Armaflex) — MUST have vapour barrier

table.notes: Minimum for condensation prevention in plant room

cws_cold_water.l8_note: L8 ACOP requires cold water to be maintained below 20°C. In warm plant rooms or ceiling voids, thicker insulation may be needed. TIMSA guide recommends checking ambient temperature assumptions.
cws_cold_water.vapour_barrier: ESSENTIAL for cold water insulation. Elastomeric foam provides integral VB. If mineral wool is used for cold water (not recommended), a separate vapour barrier is mandatory.
cws_cold_water.flag_if: Cold water insulation specified as mineral wool without vapour barrier — flag as potential condensation risk.

chilled_water.material_default: Elastomeric foam (Armaflex) — MANDATORY closed-cell with integral VB

table.notes: Higher thickness due to greater temperature differential

chilled_water.vapour_barrier: CRITICAL. Chilled water operates below ambient dew point — condensation is guaranteed without proper vapour-sealed insulation. All joints must be glued and sealed.
chilled_water.support_inserts: Vapour-sealed pipe support inserts MANDATORY at every bracket/clip. Standard pipe clips crush elastomeric insulation and create cold bridges → condensation → dripping → ceiling damage.
flag_if: Chilled water insulation not specified as elastomeric/closed-cell — flag.
No mention of vapour barrier — flag.
No mention of support inserts — flag.

refrigerant.rule: BOTH liquid and suction lines MUST be insulated. Suction line is cold — condensation risk. Liquid line carries warm refrigerant but still needs insulation for efficiency.
refrigerant.typical_thickness: 13-19mm for most VRF/split system refrigerant pipes.
refrigerant.flag_if: Refrigerant pipework shown without insulation specification — flag. Both lines need insulation.
refrigerant.cross_ref: See KB-M03 for VRF/VRV system details.

condensate.material_default: Elastomeric foam (prevents condensation on cold condensate pipe)
condensate.note: Condensate from AHUs, FCUs, and DX units is cold. Uninsulated condensate pipes will drip. Often missed in specs — flag if not addressed.

unheated_spaces.rule: ALL pipework in unheated spaces (car parks, loading bays, external, exposed risers) requires insulation per Part L. Even CWS and waste pipes may need frost protection insulation.
unheated_spaces.frost_protection: Trace heating + insulation may be required for pipework at risk of freezing (external, unheated car parks). Cross-reference spec for trace heating requirements.
unheated_spaces.flag_if: Pipework shown in unheated space without insulation specification — flag.

examples: DN100 steel, 50mm mineral wool, foil faced with aluminium cladding
DN150 steel, 60mm rock wool, canvas faced for painting
extraction_rules: ALWAYS note all four elements: pipe diameter + insulation thickness + insulation material + facing type.
If insulation TYPE is not stated, DO NOT ASSUME — flag as "insulation type not specified".
If insulation THICKNESS is not stated but material is, cross-reference BS 5422 thickness tables above and note as "assumed per BS 5422 Table [X] — verify".
If FACING is not stated, note as "facing not specified" — flag for spec check.
Different insulation specifications = SEPARATE LINE ITEMS (e.g. LTHW 25mm MW and LTHW 40mm MW are two separate items).
Measure in linear metres (m) by pipe size and insulation specification.
Each unique combination of pipe size + insulation thickness + material + facing = one line item.
notation.line_item_rule: A line item should read: "[Qty] m of [insulation thickness]mm [material] [facing] insulation to [pipe diameter]mm [pipe material] [service type] pipework". Example: "150m of 25mm phenolic foil-faced insulation to 22mm copper LTHW pipework".

valve_fitting_insulation.rule: Valves and fittings require more insulation material than straight pipe due to their irregular shape. Count as nr items with a multiplier on the linear pipe insulation rate.

valve.name: Valve Insulation
valve.description: Allow 1.5× the linear pipe section rate per valve.
valve.method: Pre-formed valve box (factory-made) or site-fabricated from sheet insulation.
valve.count_rule: Count every insulated valve as 1nr. Note pipe size and insulation spec. Rate at 1.5× the per-metre insulation rate for that pipe size.
valve.note: Not all valves are insulated — isolation valves that need frequent access may be left uninsulated with removable insulation jacket. Check spec.

flange.name: Flange Insulation
flange.description: Allow 2× the linear pipe section rate per flange pair.
flange.method: Pre-formed flange box or removable flange insulation jacket.
flange.count_rule: Count every insulated flange pair as 1nr. Note pipe size. Rate at 2× the per-metre rate.
flange.note: Flanges are bulky — more material and labour to insulate than valves.

valve_box.name: Pre-Formed Valve Insulation Box
valve_box.description: Factory-made insulation enclosure shaped to fit standard valve body. Hinged or removable for maintenance access.
valve_box.count_rule: Count as nr if specified. State pipe size and valve type.
valve_box.note: Pre-formed boxes are neater and provide better insulation performance than site-fabricated. More expensive. Check spec for preference.

strainer_box.name: Strainer Insulation Box
strainer_box.description: Removable insulation box for Y-strainer or basket strainer. Must be removable for strainer cleaning.
strainer_box.count_rule: Count as nr. State pipe size.
strainer_box.note: Strainer boxes MUST be removable for maintenance access. Always removable type.

removable_jacket.name: Removable Insulation Jacket / Mattress
removable_jacket.description: Reusable insulation jacket with Velcro or toggle fastening. For equipment, valves, and fittings that need regular maintenance access.
applications: Large valves where insulation removal is frequent
removable_jacket.count_rule: Count as nr. State size/equipment. Measured by manufacturer from site survey.
removable_jacket.note: Removable jackets are significantly more expensive than permanent insulation. Only specified for items needing regular access.

implicit_items: If pipe insulation is specified, valve and fitting insulation is ALWAYS required unless spec explicitly excludes it.
If no valve insulation is mentioned in spec, include it as "Implicit" source with a note that it is standard practice.
Fittings (elbows, tees) in straight pipe runs are typically included in the linear metre rate — only count separately if pre-formed fitting covers are specified.
Valve insulation on cold/chilled services is CRITICAL — uninsulated valves are cold bridges that cause condensation and dripping.

waste_factor.pipe_sections: 15% — for offcuts, fitting covers, joint overlaps.
waste_factor.sheet_material: 12% — for wrapping valves, vessels, irregular shapes.
waste_factor.note: Cross-reference KB-C02 for detailed waste factor tables.

accessories.adhesive: Manufacturer adhesive for elastomeric joints. Allow 1 tin per ~30-40m of pipe insulation.
accessories.tape: Foil tape for sealing joints on foil-faced insulation. Self-amalgamating tape for outer protection.
accessories.pins_and_clips: Insulation support pins (for mineral wool on ducts/vessels). Stainless steel pins with speed washers.
accessories.banding: Stainless steel or aluminium banding for securing insulation and cladding. Allow 2 bands per metre of cladding.
accessories.vapour_seal_tape: Vapour barrier tape for sealing joints on cold-service insulation. CRITICAL for chilled water systems.
accessories.support_inserts: Pre-formed support inserts for pipe brackets on insulated cold pipes. One per bracket. Essential to prevent cold bridging.
accessories.note: Accessories are often included in the insulation rate per metre. Check pricing method. If accessories are priced separately, count them.

flag_triggers: Insulation material not specified — flag. Do not assume material type.
Insulation thickness not specified — flag. Note BS 5422 default and label as "assumed".
Cold water insulation specified as mineral wool without vapour barrier — flag condensation risk.
Chilled water system without closed-cell (elastomeric) insulation — flag as non-compliant with best practice.
Chilled water insulation without support inserts mentioned — flag cold bridging risk.
Refrigerant pipework without insulation — flag. Both lines must be insulated.
Condensate pipework without insulation — flag. Will cause dripping.
Pipework in unheated space without insulation — flag frost risk.
Fire rating of insulation not stated for areas requiring fire performance (escape routes, plenums) — flag.
Insulation to existing pipework specified — flag. May need removal of old insulation first (asbestos risk if pre-2000 building).
Aluminium cladding specified — count as separate item (m² or m). Not included in basic insulation rate.
